compiled_state.title: PreBash hook: direct record-test-status.sh call by agent -> no guard/block
compiled_state.author: Test
compiled_state.description:
## Expected Behavior
A PreToolUse/PreBash hook detects when a Bash command invokes record-test-status.sh directly and blocks the call, explaining that the script must run via the pre-commit hook (not manually) to ensure the correct staged-file context.

## Actual Behavior
No guard exists. Agents can call record-test-status.sh directly, which runs outside pre-commit hook context — potentially on the wrong diff hash or wrong staged-file set — producing stale or incorrect test-gate-status entries that later gate commits incorrectly.

## Steps to Reproduce
1. Agent stages files
2. Agent calls `bash plugins/dso/hooks/record-test-status.sh` directly in a Bash tool call
3. Script runs successfully with no warning

## Impact
Test gate can be satisfied with a status recorded against a mismatched diff, allowing commits to pass the gate without valid test coverage for the actual staged diff.

comments.body: Classification: behavioral, Score: 2 (BASIC) — Gate 1b required (potential feature request)
comments.author: Test

comments.body: Gate 1a: intent-aligned (high confidence) — missing guard is a genuine defect, not a documented design decision
comments.author: Test
